subject: [PATCH] mm/mempolicy: Fix weighted interleave auto sysfs name

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary:
Reviewer expressed disappointment and questioned whether the patch addressed all instances of 'auto' in the sysfs structure, implying that further investigation is needed.

Reviewer Gregory Price expressed uncertainty about the patch, but provided a Reviewed-by tag indicating they reviewed it.
reviews.raw_body:
Well that's really unfortunately.

Is this really the only "auto" in the entire sysfs structure?

---

Maybe - 

Fixes: 2fb6915fa22d ("compiler_types.h: add "auto" as a macro for "__auto_type"")


Reviewed-by: Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net>

~Gregory

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that passing 'auto' as a raw token to __ATTR macro was problematic and agreed it should be checked for in other macros, but did not confirm if the patch will need further revision.

Author initially decided against adding a Fixes tag, but then reconsidered and suggested adding it to the patch that introduced the sysfs file instead.

Author acknowledged that the feedback was about the sysfs name being expanded due to the __auto_type macro and agreed to directly pass a string 'auto' instead of the raw token.
reviews.raw_body:
Thankfully, it seems so:

$ rg "__ATTR\(auto"

mm/mempolicy.c
3786:   __ATTR(auto, 0664, weighted_interleave_auto_show,

arch/powerpc/platforms/pseries/power.c
44:     __ATTR(auto_poweron, 0644, auto_poweron_show, auto_poweron_store);

Although maybe it is more valuable to check any __ATTR family macro
for a macro as the first parameter.  In hindsight, I should have been more
wary of passing a keyword as a token.

[...snip...]

---

I thought about adding a Fixes tag, decided against it because it's
purely cosmetic.

... but now that I think about it, I think this could break some workflows
for users who have automated writing to the auto sysfs file.

Maybe I should add a fixes tag for the patch that introduced the 
sysfs file instead? That is,

Fixes: e341f9c3c841 ("mm/mempolicy: Weighted Interleave Auto-tuning")

---

Thanks again, Gregory!! I hope you have a great day : -)
Joshua

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch breaks userland software relying on the __auto_type symbol, and expressed concern about the potential impact of this change.

Reviewer Gregory Price noted that the original commit introduced 'auto' and a later commit modified it to '__auto_type', which broke userland, making the latter the correct commit to mark for Fixes.
sentiment_signals: potential breakage for dependent userland software
reviews.raw_body:
Yes, it broke userland software depending on that symbol being there
(which, hopefully, is very little at this point in time).

---

The original commit introduced 'auto' and the compiler_types.h commit
modified that to "__auto_type".  The latter broke userland so that's the
right commit to mark for Fixes.

~Gregory

2026-04-07.patch_summary: This patch fixes a naming issue in the weighted interleave auto sysfs attribute by directly passing a string 'auto' instead of the raw token, which was being expanded to __auto_type. This change prevents the sysfs entry from displaying as __auto_type and ensures it shows as 'auto'. The patch also bypasses an unnecessary permissions check for this specific file. The fix is based on a recent commit in the mm-new branch.

reviews.author: Rakie Kim
reviews.summary: Reviewer Rakie Kim acknowledged that Joshua Hahn's patch was a good catch to resolve an unexpected global macro expansion issue, and approved the fix.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Joshua,

This was indeed an unfortunate and tricky issue to track down, given
that it was caused by an unexpected global macro expansion. great catch,
and thanks for the quick response and clean fix.

Reviewed-by: Rakie Kim <rakie.kim@sk.com>

reviews.author: Zi Yan
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch description does not match the actual changes made to the code, specifically pointing out that the original commit message mentioned using __ATTR macro but the current patch bypasses VERIFY_OCTAL_PERMISSIONS check.
reviews.raw_body:
Acked-by: Zi Yan <ziy@nvidia.com>

Best Regards,
Yan, Zi

2026-04-08.patch_summary: This patch fixes a naming issue in the weighted interleave auto sysfs attribute by directly passing a string 'auto' instead of the raw token, which was being expanded to __auto_type. This change prevents the sysfs entry from displaying as __auto_type and ensures it shows as 'auto'. The patch also bypasses an unnecessary permissions check for this specific file. The fix is based on a recent commit in the mm-new branch.

reviews.author: Huang, Ying
reviews.summary: Reviewer requested that Fixes tag be added to the commit message for easier backporting and suggested adding Acked-by: Huang Ying in future versions
reviews.raw_body:
Fixes tag helps people to decide whether to backport the commit.  So, I
think that we need it anyway.  Feel free to add

Acked-by: Huang Ying <ying.huang@linux.alibaba.com>

in the future versions.

[snip]

---
Best Regards,
Huang, Ying
